title: The Daily Foot Check
body: A daily routine for looking at both feet: top, bottom, between toes, heels. Building a photo baseline so you can compare day over day. Why routine beats intensity.

title: Skin Care and Moisture
body: Dry skin, cracking, and fissures on heels and soles. How to moisturize safely, what to avoid between toes, and when dryness deserves a mention to your care team.

title: Foot Photos That Actually Help
body: How to take a useful foot photo: lighting near a window, camera distance, tap to focus, capturing plantar and dorsal views, keeping a consistent angle for comparison over time. A good baseline photo makes every future check better.

title: Redness, Pressure, and Footwear Fit
body: Red areas that appear where shoes press. Checking footwear fit, socks without seams, pressure points, and how irritation shows up before bigger problems.

title: Swelling: What to Watch
body: Swelling and edema in feet and ankles. Comparing one side to the other, tracking changes across days, elevation basics, and when swelling is worth a call.

title: Blisters, Cuts, and Skin Breaks
body: Why any skin break, wound, blister, or cut on a diabetic foot deserves prompt attention from your care team. Keeping it clean and covered in the meantime. Never ignore a break in the skin.

title: Toenails and Nail Changes
body: Toenail thickening, color changes, ingrown edges. Safe trimming basics and which nail changes to bring up at your next visit.

title: Calluses and Corns
body: How friction builds callus and corns, why self-cutting is risky with diabetes, and gentler options while you wait to see a professional.

title: Color and Circulation
body: Discoloration, dusky or pale color changes, temperature differences between feet, and why circulation changes are worth showing your care team.

title: Comparing Left and Right
body: Asymmetry as a signal: using your baseline photos to compare feet against each other and against last week. Small consistent comparisons catch changes early.
